text.title: Link Building Framework
text.summary: Value-first link building: create link-worthy content, identify prospects, conduct personalized outreach, track and iterate.

rule.statement: Create content that journalists and bloggers need but that doesn't yet exist or is outdated — this is the only reliably scalable link magnet formula.
rule.rationale: Generic content earns generic results. Link magnets are assets people reference repeatedly: original data, free tools, comprehensive guides, industry benchmarks. Without a genuinely link-worthy asset, outreach volume cannot compensate.

list.item: Original research / surveys: Very High link potential
Free interactive tools / calculators: Very High link potential
Industry benchmarks with data: Very High link potential
Ultimate / comprehensive guides: High link potential
Expert roundups: Medium link potential
Case studies with real data: Medium link potential
Original graphics / infographics: Medium link potential

rule.statement: Build a prospect list of 100+ sites before starting outreach — quality and volume both matter.
rule.rationale: Response rates average 10-20%; link acquisition rate averages 5-10%. Starting with fewer than 100 prospects produces statistically insignificant results and cannot reveal which tactics work.

list.item: Competitor backlink analysis (Ahrefs): who links to competing content
Resource page search: "[topic] + resources" / "[topic] + useful links"
Broken link building: find broken links, offer replacement
HARO / Qwoted: respond to journalist queries (High quality, inbound)
Unlinked brand mentions: find mentions without links, request attribution
Skyscraper technique: find linked content, create superior version

rule.statement: Personalize every outreach email — generic emails are ignored at rates above 90%.
rule.rationale: Outreach recipients receive dozens of generic link requests daily. A personalized first line referencing specific content they published signals genuine effort and increases response rates from ~2% (generic) to ~15-20% (personalized).

rule.statement: Follow up exactly once, 7 days after the initial send — never follow up more than once.
rule.rationale: A single follow-up recovers responses from busy recipients who missed the first email. Multiple follow-ups damage relationship and sender reputation without proportional benefit.

list.item: Send volume: 50 emails per day maximum to protect sender reputation
Personalization: read 2-3 recent articles, reference specific content in first line
Value framing: explain why linking benefits THEIR audience, not just you
Easy ask: provide direct link, short description, specific reason to include

rule.statement: Audit internal links monthly: link new posts to relevant old content, update old posts to link to new content, build pillar-cluster structures with keyword-rich anchors.
rule.rationale: Internal links are the highest-ROI link building tactic — zero outreach effort, immediate implementation, direct PageRank distribution, and visible ranking improvements within weeks not months.

list.item: Response rate: 10% = good, 20%+ = great
Link acquisition rate: 5% = good, 10%+ = great
Average DR of new links: 40+ = good, 60+ = great
New referring domains per month: 5+ = good, 20+ = great

antipattern.description: Buying links or using private blog networks (PBNs)
antipattern.reason: Google manual and algorithmic penalties — site-wide ranking loss that can take months to recover from. The short-term ranking boost is not worth the existential risk.

antipattern.description: Sending generic outreach at scale without personalization
antipattern.reason: Response rates drop below 2%, burning prospect lists and sender domain reputation simultaneously.

text.reference: Personalized email templates for resource pages, broken links, guest posts
Campaign tracking template with metrics and weekly process
